description: Mobile deep-link surface: Android intent:// URIs + intent-filter exported activities, iOS universal links (apple-app-site-association), iOS custom URL schemes, and Flutter / React Native router parameter injection. Reference-only — requires device + manifest inspection. Maps to OWASP Mobile Top 10 2024 M4 (Insufficient Input/Output Validation) + HackTricks Mobile section.

android_implicit_intent_redirect.description: App accepts intent:// URI from third-party (browser, other app) and forwards as Intent. If extras carry attacker-controlled component name → arbitrary activity launch with attacker extras.

probes.description: Browser → intent → app: extras.url passed to WebView.loadUrl. javascript: scheme → JS execution inside app's WebView session.
Explicit component target — bypasses intent-filter restrictions if activity not declared android:exported='false'
Force WebView activity to load local file URL — exfil app's own SQLite databases if file:// access enabled

android_exported_activity_takeover.description: Exported activity (android:exported='true' OR has intent-filter — implicit-exported pre-API-31) callable from any other app on device. Privilege boundary == app sandbox boundary.

probes.description: Launch activity with attacker URL. Use adb when device authorised; equivalent crafted intent: in malicious app context.

probes.payload: adb shell am start -n com.victim.app/.export.ExportActivity --es file '../../shared_prefs/auth.xml'
probes.description: Pass extras with traversal — if activity reads --es file and reflects/exports, app's own auth.xml leaks

android_custom_scheme_takeover.description: App registers myapp:// scheme + same scheme is registered by another app installed on device — first app to intent-filter handles. Pre-fillable URLs in browser bookmark / web link → install hijacker.

probes.description: OAuth callback hijack — attacker app intent-filters myapp://oauth/* and steals authorization code en route to victim app

ios_universal_link_misconfig.description: iOS universal links via apple-app-site-association (AASA) file at /.well-known/apple-app-site-association — if path patterns too permissive ("*"), attacker can register a sibling AASA on a subdomain they control and intercept.

probes.description: Fetch AASA — examine applinks.details[].paths. Wildcards like ['*'] or ['/oauth/*'] are red flags.
Legacy path (pre iOS 9.3) — sometimes still served alongside the .well-known one

ios_custom_url_scheme.description: iOS custom URL schemes (myapp://) are NOT unique-registered — multiple apps can claim same scheme. App must verify caller via LSApplicationQueriesSchemes + handleOpenURL source-app validation.

probes.description: Crafted callback — works the same way as Android custom scheme but with iOS's last-installed-wins behaviour.

android_app_links_signature_required.description: Android App Links via /.well-known/assetlinks.json (Digital Asset Links). Misconfigured assetlinks.json (wrong SHA-256 fingerprint, missing entries for staging variants) allows attacker app to claim links.

probes.description: Fetch assetlinks.json — verify sha256_cert_fingerprints field present and matches victim app signing cert

rn_flutter_deeplink_router.description: React Native and Flutter apps consume deep-link parameters in JS/Dart router code. Often pass user-controlled string to navigator.push without route allowlist — opens any registered route + attacker-controlled args.

probes.description: Deep link forces app to admin route with attacker user_id — privilege escalation if route lacks server-side check

deeplink_dangerous_intent_actions.description: Android intent actions worth exploring on exported components: SEND, SEND_MULTIPLE (file share), VIEW (web URL), EDIT (in-place edit), PICK (chooser). Each accepts EXTRA_STREAM URI which webviews/file-readers often resolve naively.

probes.payload: adb shell am start -a android.intent.action.SEND -t text/plain --eu android.intent.extra.STREAM 'file:///etc/hosts' -n com.victim.app/.share.ShareActivity
probes.description: EXTRA_STREAM with file:// URI pointing at sensitive file — if share activity reads stream content and exfils, content disclosure
